metadata_information.title: Monetary Policy in Fossil Fuel Exporters : The Curse of Horizons
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Monetary Policy in Fossil Fuel Exporters : The Curse of Horizons

document_description.abstract: This paper examines the role of monetary policy in fossil fuel exporters at different horizons. The main argument is that central banks in these economies need to look beyond the horizon of the business cycle. In the short run, (independent) monetary policy should flexibly target inflation. In the medium run, central banks need to coordinate with fiscal authorities to ensure that monetary policy operates around a credible and sustainable fiscal anchor. In the long run, central banks should beware of the existential threats posed by new risks related to stranded assets.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
